821	\subsection{The affine base-change lemma and its remaining obligations}
822	
823	The affine base-change lemma below proceeds \emph{directly on global sections}: it
824	reduces, via the locality criterion
825	Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} and the two affine dictionaries
826	Lemmas~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso} and~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso},
827	to two obligations that are isolated here as named blocks. Both are extracted from
828	the proof of Stacks ``Affine base change'', and both are absent from the pinned
829	Mathlib. The first is the \emph{locality reduction}: restricting to the affine opens
830	of \(S'\) reduces the isomorphism claim to the affine--affine model
831	\(S = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\), \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\),
832	\(X = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\), \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\). The second is the
833	\emph{section-level identification}: in that affine--affine model, the global-sections
834	incarnation \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) of the base-change map, transported through the two
835	proved tilde dictionaries, is the canonical cancellation isomorphism
836	\(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) — an isomorphism with no flatness hypothesis. This
837	is the honest cut of the affine close: locality on \(S'\), then a pure computation on
838	the module of global sections. No separate abstract adjoint-mate identification is
839	performed at the level of sheaves; the mate is unwound \emph{on sections}, where it
840	becomes \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) directly.
841	
842	\begin{lemma}
843	
844	\leanok
845	[Affine-local compatibility of the base-change map]
846	  \label{lem:base_change_map_affine_local}
847	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_map_affine_local}
848	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
849	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
850	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
851	  % "local on $S$ and $S'$" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
852	  % L920--926).
853	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "The statement is local on $S$ and $S'$. Hence we may
854	  % assume $X = \Spec(A)$, $S = \Spec(R)$,
855	  % $S' = \Spec(R')$ and $\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}$
856	  % for some $A$-module $M$."
857	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
858	  change'' (proof, local-on-\(S\)-and-\(S'\) step).}
859	  Let \(f : X \to S\) be an affine morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent
860	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module, with a base-change square as above. The formation of the
861	  base-change map (Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}) is compatible
862	  with restriction to affine opens of \(S'\): for each affine open \(U \subseteq S'\), the
863	  restriction of the base-change map to sections over \(U\) agrees with the
864	  base-change map of the square obtained by restricting along \(U\). Consequently,
865	  by the affine-open locality criterion
866	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the assertion that the base-change
867	  map is an isomorphism over an arbitrary base-change square \((S, S', X, X')\)
868	  reduces to the affine--affine case \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(R)\),
869	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\), \(X = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
870	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\).
871	
872	\end{lemma}
873	
874	\begin{proof}
875	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}
876	  By the affine-open locality criterion
877	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens}, the base-change map is an isomorphism
878	  of \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules as soon as its restriction to the sections over each
879	  affine open \(U \subseteq S'\) is an isomorphism. The only content beyond the bare criterion is to identify that
880	  restriction with the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out over
881	  \(U\). We obtain the identification by unfolding the definition of the map and
882	  pushing the restriction functor through each of its building blocks. The
883	  compatibility this needs is \emph{not} a new coherence theorem: it is the naturality
884	  of the adjunction transpose, together with the standard fact that pushforward
885	  commutes with restriction to an open of the base. We spell this out in three steps.
886	
887	  \medskip
888	  \emph{Step 1: unfold the map.} By
889	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map}, the base-change map is the image,
890	  under the \((g^*, g_*)\)-adjunction transpose — the natural bijection
891	  \(\operatorname{Hom}(g^*A, B) \cong \operatorname{Hom}(A, g_*B)\) — of the morphism
892	  \[
893	    f_*\mathcal{F} \xrightarrow{\,f_*(\eta_{\mathcal{F}})\,}
894	    f_*(g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F} = g_*\,f'_*(g')^*\mathcal{F},
895	  \]
896	  where \(\eta_{\mathcal{F}} : \mathcal{F} \to (g')_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}\) is the unit of
897	  the \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction and the identification
898	  \(f_*(g')_* = g_*\,f'_*\) is the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward applied to the
899	  square's commutativity \(g \circ f' = f \circ g'\). Thus the map is, by
900	  construction, the transpose of the \(f_*\)-image of a unit — a composite of
901	  adjunction data and pushforward functors, with no ingredient beyond these.
902	
903	  \medskip
904	  \emph{Step 2: restriction to \(U\) commutes with each building block.} Fix an affine
905	  open \(U \subseteq S'\) and write \(\rho_U\) for restriction to \(U\) —
906	  equivalently, the section functor \(\Gamma(U, -)\) on \(\mathcal{O}_{S'}\)-modules,
907	  or pullback along the open immersion \(U \hookrightarrow S'\). Restriction along
908	  \(U\) is a functor, and every ingredient of Step 1 is a natural transformation or a
909	  natural bijection; so applying \(\rho_U\) to the transpose-of-a-unit of Step 1
910	  returns the transpose-of-the-unit built from the restricted data. Concretely:
911	  \begin{itemize}
912	    \item[(a)] the unit \(\eta_{\mathcal{F}}\) of the \(((g')^*, (g')_*)\)-adjunction
913	      restricts to the unit of the corresponding adjunction on the restricted square,
914	      because units are natural in their object and pulling the cartesian square back
915	      along \(U \hookrightarrow S'\) yields again a cartesian square (with total space
916	      the open \((f')^{-1}(U)\) of \(X'\));
917	    \item[(b)] \(f_*\) of a restricted morphism is the restriction of \(f_*\) of the
918	      morphism, because pushforward commutes with restriction to an open of the base:
919	      for an open \(U\) of the base one has
920	      \((f_*\mathcal{G})|_U = (f|_{f^{-1}U})_*\bigl(\mathcal{G}|_{f^{-1}U}\bigr)\),
921	      naturally in \(\mathcal{G}\) — and likewise for \((g')_*\) and \(f'_*\);
922	    \item[(c)] the \((g^*, g_*)\)-adjunction transpose is a bijection natural in both
923	      variables, so it commutes with restricting its source and target along \(U\).
924	  \end{itemize}
925	  Chaining (a), (b), (c) — i.e.\ transporting the transpose-of-unit composite of
926	  Step 1 through the restriction functor \(\rho_U\) and replacing each block by its
927	  restricted counterpart — shows that the restriction of the base-change map to
928	  sections over \(U\) is itself the base-change map formed from the cartesian square
929	  restricted over \(U\). Choosing an affine open
930	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing the image \(g(U)\), affineness of
931	  \(f\) (local on the base and stable under base change) makes the total space
932	  restrict to an affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\), and quasi-coherence of
933	  \(\mathcal{F}\) makes it restrict to a tilde-module \(\widetilde{M}\) there; so this
934	  restricted map is exactly the base-change map of the affine--affine square cut out
935	  over \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R')\) and \(\operatorname{Spec}(R)\). The whole
936	  identification is thus definitional plus the naturalities (a)--(c); it introduces no
937	  obligation beyond them.
938	
939	  \medskip
940	  \emph{Step 3: conclude the reduction.} By Step 2 the per-affine-open hypothesis
941	  required by the locality criterion Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} —
942	  that the base-change map restricts to an isomorphism on sections over each affine
943	  open \(U \subseteq S'\) — is, under the identification of Step 2, precisely the
944	  affine--affine section assertion. That assertion is supplied, with \emph{no} flatness
945	  hypothesis, by the section-level computation
946	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}, which identifies the
947	  affine--affine base-change map on global sections with the cancellation isomorphism
948	  \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\). Applying the locality criterion
949	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} to this family of per-open
950	  isomorphisms shows that the base-change map is an isomorphism, as asserted.
951	\end{proof}
952	
953	\subsection{The section-level base-change identity}
954	
955	The section-level identification

9	/-!
10	# Flat base change for the pushforward of a quasi-coherent sheaf (`i = 0`)
11	
12	This file establishes the `i = 0` (direct-image) case of flat base change: the
13	formation of the pushforward `f_* F` of a quasi-coherent sheaf commutes with flat
14	base change on the target.
15	
16	Throughout we work with a (commutative, eventually cartesian) square of schemes
17	```
18	  X' --g'--> X
19	  |f'        |f
20	  v          v
21	  S' --g---> S
22	```
23	recorded by morphisms `f : X ⟶ S`, `g : S' ⟶ S`, `f' : X' ⟶ S'`, `g' : X' ⟶ X`
24	with `g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`, and `F : X.Modules` a sheaf of modules on `X`.
25	
26	The three main declarations are:
27	
28	* `AlgebraicGeometry.pushforwardBaseChangeMap` — the canonical base-change map
29	  `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)`, built as the adjoint mate of the unit of the
30	  `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction.
31	* `AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` — for `f` affine and the
32	  square cartesian, the base-change map is an isomorphism (affine case: tensor
33	  associativity).
34	* `AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` — for `g` flat and `f`
35	  quasi-compact quasi-separated, the base-change map is an isomorphism.
36	
37	See `blueprint/src/chapters/Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex`.
38	
39	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
40	Tag 02KH.
41	-/
42	
43	universe u
44	
45	open CategoryTheory Limits
46	
47	namespace AlgebraicGeometry
48	
49	open Scheme.Modules
50	
51	open scoped TensorProduct
52	
53	variable {S S' X X' : Scheme.{u}}
54	  (f : X ⟶ S) (g : S' ⟶ S) (f' : X' ⟶ S') (g' : X' ⟶ X)
55	
56	/-- The canonical base-change map for the pushforward of a sheaf of modules.
57	
58	Given a commutative square
59	```
60	  X' --g'--> X
61	  |f'        |f
62	  v          v
63	  S' --g---> S
64	```
65	(with `comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g`) and a sheaf of modules `F` on `X`, this is the
66	canonical morphism `g^*(f_* F) ⟶ f'_*((g')^* F)` of sheaves of modules on `S'`.
67	
68	It is the image, under the `(g^*, g_*)`-adjunction transpose, of the composite
69	```
70	  f_* F --f_*(unit)--> f_* (g')_* (g')^* F
71	        = (g' ≫ f)_* (g')^* F = (f' ≫ g)_* (g')^* F = g_* f'_* (g')^* F,
72	```
73	where `unit` is the unit of the `((g')^*, (g')_*)`-adjunction and the middle
74	equalities are the pseudofunctoriality of pushforward together with the
75	commutativity of the square.
76	
77	Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, §"Cohomology and base change, I",
78	base-change diagram. -/
79	noncomputable def pushforwardBaseChangeMap (comm : g' ≫ f = f' ≫ g) (F : X.Modules) :
80	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj ((pushforward f).obj F) ⟶
81	      (pushforward f').obj ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) :=
82	  ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g).homEquiv _ _).symm
83	    ((pushforward f).map ((pullbackPushforwardAdjunction g').unit.app F) ≫
84	      (pushforwardComp g' f).hom.app _ ≫
85	      (pushforwardCongr comm).hom.app _ ≫
86	      (pushforwardComp f' g).inv.app _)
87

997	  \label{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}

1236	% --- Supporting lemmas for the codomain read ---
1237	% These helpers establish that pullback along a scheme isomorphism is an equivalence
1238	% of module categories; the adjunction unit of an equivalence is an isomorphism,
1239	% used in the codomain-read computation.
1240	
1241	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1242	[Pullback along an isomorphism is an equivalence of module categories]
1243	  \label{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1244	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1245	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) be an isomorphism of schemes. Then the pullback functor on
1246	  \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is
1247	  one half of an equivalence of categories
1248	  \(Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \simeq X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\), with quasi-inverse
1249	  \((f^{-1})^*\).
1250	\end{lemma}
1251	\begin{proof}
1252	
1253	  Assemble the equivalence from the functor \(f^*\) and the candidate inverse
1254	  \((f^{-1})^*\). The unit and counit isomorphisms are built from the pseudofunctor
1255	  coherences of pullback: \((f^{-1})^* \circ f^* \cong (f \circ f^{-1})^* = \mathrm{id}^*
1256	  \cong \mathrm{id}\) and symmetrically, using
1257	  \(f \circ f^{-1} = \mathrm{id}_Y\), \(f^{-1} \circ f = \mathrm{id}_X\)
1258	  (composition-of-pullbacks, congruence under equal maps, and pullback-of-identity).
1259	  The triangle identities are forced by these coherences.
1260	\end{proof}
1261	
1262	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1263	[The pullback functor along an isomorphism is an equivalence]
1264	  \label{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1265	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}
1266	  \uses{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso}
1267	  Consequently, for an isomorphism \(f : X \to Y\) the functor
1268	  \(f^* : Y\text{-}\mathbf{Mod} \to X\text{-}\mathbf{Mod}\) is an equivalence of
1269	  categories. In particular its adjunction unit is a natural isomorphism, so
1270	  \((\eta_{f})_{W}\) is invertible on every object \(W\).
1271	\end{lemma}
1272	\begin{proof}
1273	
1274	  The functor underlying the equivalence of
1275	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackIsoEquivalenceOfIso} is exactly \(f^*\), so \(f^*\) is an
1276	  equivalence; an equivalence's unit is a natural isomorphism.
1277	\end{proof}

1283	\begin{lemma}\leanok
1284	[Regrouping isomorphism for the section-level mate]
1285	  \label{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1286	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}
1287	  % NOTE: tensor-order convention. The Lean uses Mathlib's `pullbackSpecIso`
1288	  % orientation `A ⊗[R] R'` (so the codomain is `(A ⊗_R R') ⊗_A M` and the generator
1289	  % goes `r' ⊗ m ↦ (1 ⊗ r') ⊗ m`); the prose below writes `R' ⊗_R A`. The two are the
1290	  % same module up to `TensorProduct.comm` — a faithful re-orientation, no content change.
1291	  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1292	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
1293	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I), proof, the
1294	  % "boils down to the equality" step (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex,
1295	  % L933--938).
1296	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
1297	  % equality
1298	  % $$
1299	  % (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
1300	  % $$
1301	  % as $R'$-modules."
1302	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
1303	  change'' (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1304	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\) and \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms and \(M\) an
1305	  \(A\)-module. There is a bundled \(R'\)-linear isomorphism
1306	  \[
1307	    \operatorname{regroup} :
1308	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M ,
1309	  \]
1310	  the ``equality as \(R'\)-modules'' of the source, sending the generator
1311	  \((r' \otimes 1) \otimes m \mapsto r' \otimes m\); equivalently its inverse sends
1312	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto (r' \otimes 1) \otimes m\). It carries \emph{no} flatness
1313	  hypothesis.
1314	\end{lemma}
1315	
1316	\begin{proof}
1317	  \uses{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}
1318	  The underlying \(R'\)-linear equivalence is supplied, already as a bundled
1319	  \(\operatorname{LinearEquiv}\) over \(R'\), by the natively \(R'\)-linear
1320	  pushout-cancellation equivalence of
1321	  Lemma~\ref{lem:isPushout_cancelBaseChange_mathlib}. Crucially, that helper bundles
1322	  \(R'\)-linearity \emph{internally}: there is no hand-written scalar-compatibility
1323	  obligation, no induction over elementary tensors, and in particular \emph{no} residual
1324	  \(r' \cdot 0 = 0\) bookkeeping branch. The only work left to this proof is therefore to
1325	  promote that \(R'\)-linear equivalence to a morphism of \(R'\)-module \emph{objects} in
1326	  the exact extension/restriction-of-scalars packaging that the codomain and domain reads
1327	  produce. We work in the \(A \otimes_R R'\) orientation
1328	  (the same module as \(R' \otimes_R A\) up to the canonical commutativity isomorphism,
1329	  per the tensor-order convention note).
1330	
1331	  \emph{The lone residual obstruction is the \(A\)-action diamond, resolved at the object
1332	  level.} The pushout-cancellation helper takes its relative tensor product over the
1333	  \emph{canonical} \(A\)-algebra structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\)
1334	  (multiplication in the left factor), whereas the carrier object produced by the
1335	  dictionaries,
1336	  the extension of scalars of \(M\) along the left inclusion \(\iota : A \to A \otimes_R R'\),
1337	  tensors over the \(A\)-module structure on \(A \otimes_R R'\) induced by \(\iota\). These
1338	  two \(A\)-module structures on
1339	  \(A \otimes_R R'\) agree on elements but are distinct as data, so the two relative
1340	  tensor products are distinct types even though their underlying abelian groups and
1341	  their \(R'\)-actions coincide. This diamond is the \emph{only} thing the helper does
1342	  not already discharge, and — because the \(R'\)-linearity is now entirely internal to
1343	  the Mathlib equivalence — it is resolved purely at the carrier level by an
1344	  identity-on-elements \(R'\)-linear bridge
1345	  \[
1346	    eT : (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\operatorname{restr}} M
1347	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
1348	    (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A^{\,\mathrm{can}} M ,
1349	  \]
1350	  the identity map on generators, which is \(R'\)-linear because both sides carry the
1351	  same \(R'\)-action (restriction of scalars along \(R' \to A \otimes_R R'\)).
1352	
1353	  Composing the bridge \(eT\) with the natively \(R'\)-linear helper equivalence and
1354	  promoting to an isomorphism of module objects yields the desired bundled
1355	  \(R'\)-linear isomorphism of \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\) objects. Being a composite
1356	  of linear equivalences it is an \(R'\)-linear isomorphism, and none of its constituents
1357	  carries a flatness hypothesis.
1358	\end{proof}

1366	\begin{lemma}

1373	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma `lemma-affine-base-change'

1375	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L932--937).
1376	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the equality

1378	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base change''
1379	  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
1380	  Let \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) be the canonical inclusion
1381	  (\(a \mapsto a \otimes 1\)). The unit of the
1382	  \(\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^*, (\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_*\bigr)\)-adjunction
1383	  evaluated at the affine sheaf \(\widetilde M\), read on the global sections over
1384	  \(\operatorname{Spec} A\) through the tilde dictionaries
1385	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}, Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}) and

1387	  extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\iota_A\), namely
1388	  \(\eta_M : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\).

1390	\begin{proof}

1392	  This is the affine, square-free heart of the computation: there is no pullback square and
1393	  no base-change map yet, only the comparison of two units of two adjunctions related by the
1394	  two tilde dictionaries. The geometric adjunction
1395	  \(\bigl((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^* \dashv (\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_*\bigr)\) on
1396	  sheaves of modules transports, under the tilde dictionaries which identify
1397	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)^*\) of a tilde with extension of scalars
1398	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso}) and \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_*\) of a tilde
1399	  with restriction of scalars (Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}), to the algebraic
1400	  extension/restriction adjunction \((\iota_A^{\,*} \dashv \iota_{A\,*})\) on
1401	  \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}\). The dictionaries being the comparison isomorphisms of these
1402	  adjunctions, naturality identifies the two units; evaluating at \(M\) gives the stated
1403	  value \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\).
1404	\end{proof}

1406	\begin{definition}
1407	
1408	\leanok
1409	[The inner comparison value \(\rho\)]
1410	  \label{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
1411	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.base_change_mate_inner_value}
1412	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value, lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}
1413	  Let \(\psi : R \to R'\), \(\varphi : R \to A\) be ring homomorphisms, \(M\) an
1414	  \(A\)-module, and \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\), \(\iota_{R'} : R' \to A \otimes_R R'\)
1415	  the canonical tensor inclusions (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_fst_snd_specMap_tensor}).
1416	  The \emph{inner comparison value} is the \(R\)-linear map
1417	  \[
1418	    \rho : M \longrightarrow (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M,
1419	    \qquad m \longmapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m,
1420	  \]
1421	  obtained as the restriction of scalars along \(\varphi\) of the algebraic unit
1422	  \(\eta_M\) of the extension/restriction adjunction along \(\iota_A\)
1423	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}), transported across the equality
1424	  \(\iota_A \circ \varphi = \iota_{R'} \circ \psi\).
1425	
1426	  Proved directly in Lean.
1427	\end{definition}

1433	\begin{lemma}[Unit-across-conjugate coherence]
1434	  \label{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib}
1435	  \lean{CategoryTheory.unit_conjugateEquiv}
1436	  \mathlibok
1437	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1438	  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : L_1 \dashv R_1\) and \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : L_2 \dashv R_2\) be two
1439	  adjunctions between the same pair of categories, with units \(\eta_1\) and \(\eta_2\), and let
1440	  \(\alpha : L_2 \Rightarrow L_1\) be a natural transformation of the left adjoints. Write
1441	  \(\operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1, \mathrm{adj}_2)\) for the conjugation (mate) bijection,
1442	  which carries \(\alpha\) to a natural transformation \(R_1 \Rightarrow R_2\) of the right
1443	  adjoints. Then for every object \(c\),
1444	  \[
1445	    (\eta_1)_c \circ \bigl(\operatorname{conj}(\mathrm{adj}_1, \mathrm{adj}_2)\,\alpha\bigr)_{L_1 c}
1446	      \;=\; (\eta_2)_c \circ R_2(\alpha_c) ,
1447	  \]
1448	  the transposed form of the conjugation relating the two units across the mate of \(\alpha\).
1449	\end{lemma}
1450	
1451	\begin{lemma}[Unit of a composite adjunction]
1452	  \label{lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib}
1453	  \lean{CategoryTheory.Adjunction.comp_unit_app}
1454	  \mathlibok
1455	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1456	  Let \(\mathrm{adj}_1 : F \dashv G\) (with \(F : C \to D\)) and \(\mathrm{adj}_2 : H \dashv I\)
1457	  (with \(H : D \to E\)) be composable adjunctions, with units \(\eta_1\) and \(\eta_2\). Then the
1458	  unit of the composite adjunction
1459	  \(\mathrm{adj}_1 . \mathrm{adj}_2 : (H \circ F) \dashv (G \circ I)\) is, at every object \(X\),
1460	  \[
1461	    \bigl(\mathrm{adj}_1 . \mathrm{adj}_2\bigr)\text{-unit}_X
1462	      \;=\; (\eta_1)_X \circ G\bigl((\eta_2)_{F X}\bigr).
1463	  \]
1464	\end{lemma}
1465	
1466	\begin{lemma}[The pullback--pushforward conjugate sends the pullback coherence to the pushforward coherence]
1467	  \label{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1468	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv}
1469	  \mathlibok
1470	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
1471	  Let \(f : X \to Y\) and \(g : Y \to Z\) be composable morphisms of schemes, and write
1472	  \((h^{*} \dashv h_{*})\) for the pullback--pushforward adjunction on \(\mathcal{O}\)-modules
1473	  attached to a morphism \(h\). Under the conjugation (mate) bijection between the composite
1474	  adjunction \((g^{*}\dashv g_{*}) . (f^{*}\dashv f_{*})\) and the adjunction
1475	  \(\bigl((f\circ g)^{*} \dashv (f\circ g)_{*}\bigr)\), the inverse pullback-composition coherence
1476	  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) is carried to the pushforward-composition
1477	  coherence \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{hom}}\):
1478	  \[
1479	    \operatorname{conj}\!\Bigl((g^{*}\dashv g_{*}) . (f^{*}\dashv f_{*}),\;
1480	      \bigl((f\circ g)^{*}\dashv (f\circ g)_{*}\bigr)\Bigr)
1481	      \bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\bigr)
1482	    \;=\; (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f,g})^{\mathrm{hom}}.
1483	  \]
1484	\end{lemma}
1485	
1486	\begin{lemma}
1487	
1488	\leanok
1489	[Pseudofunctoriality of the pullback--pushforward unit]
1490	  \label{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1491	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}
1492	  \uses{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib, lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib,
1493	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1494	  Let \(a : X_1 \to X_2\) and \(b : X_2 \to X_3\) be composable morphisms of schemes and let
1495	  \(N\) be an \(\mathcal{O}_{X_3}\)-module. Write \(\eta^{h}\) for the unit of the
1496	  pullback--pushforward adjunction \((h^{*}\dashv h_{*})\) attached to a morphism \(h\), and
1497	  \(\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b}\), \(\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b}\) for the pseudofunctor
1498	  composition coherences. Then
1499	  \[
1500	    \eta^{b}_{N} \;\circ\; b_{*}\bigl(\eta^{a}_{\,b^{*}N}\bigr)
1501	      \;\circ\; (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{N}
1502	    \;=\;
1503	    \eta^{a\circ b}_{N} \;\circ\;
1504	      (a\circ b)_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}_{N}\bigr),
1505	  \]
1506	  where composites are read left to right. In words: the unit of the composite morphism
1507	  \(a \circ b\), reindexed by the inverse pullback coherence, factors as the \(b\)-unit, followed
1508	  by \(b_{*}\) of the \(a\)-unit, followed by the pushforward coherence. This is the leg-reindex
1509	  engine consumed by Seam~2 (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_fstar_reindex}): with \(a\) an
1510	  isomorphism and \(b\) an affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-map it turns the generic projection-leg
1511	  unit into the affine \(\operatorname{Spec}\)-unit modulo the transparent coherences.
1512	\end{lemma}
1513	\begin{proof}
1514	  \uses{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib, lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib,
1515	        lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib}
1516	  Apply the unit-across-conjugate coherence Lemma~\ref{lem:unit_conjugateEquiv_mathlib} to the
1517	  composite adjunction \((b^{*}\dashv b_{*}) . (a^{*}\dashv a_{*})\), the adjunction
1518	  \(\bigl((a\circ b)^{*}\dashv (a\circ b)_{*}\bigr)\), and the left-adjoint transformation
1519	  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}\). This yields an identity whose right-hand factor
1520	  is the conjugate (mate) of \((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}\); by
1521	  Lemma~\ref{lem:conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv_mathlib} that mate is exactly the pushforward
1522	  coherence \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}\). Expanding the unit of the composite
1523	  adjunction by Lemma~\ref{lem:comp_unit_app_mathlib} —
1524	  \(\bigl((b^{*}\dashv b_{*}).(a^{*}\dashv a_{*})\bigr)\text{-unit}_N
1525	    = \eta^{b}_N \circ b_{*}(\eta^{a}_{b^{*}N})\) — and reassociating the resulting composite gives
1526	  the stated equation.
1527	\end{proof}

1529	% --- Seam 2, step (ii): Gamma-collapse of the transparent pushforward coherences ---
1530	% These three project-bespoke atoms record that, on global sections over Spec R, the two
1531	% pushforward-composition coherences and the pushforward-congruence coherence appearing in
1532	% the inner composite are transparent (their section value is the identity / a presheaf
1533	% transport), hence collapse under the global-sections functor.
1534	
1535	\begin{lemma}
1536	
1537	\leanok
1538	[$\Gamma$-collapse of the pushforward-composition coherence (hom factor)]
1539	  \label{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id}
1540	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id}
1541	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
1542	  Let \(a : X_1 \to X_2\) and \(b : X_2 \to \operatorname{Spec} R\) be composable morphisms of
1543	  schemes and \(M\) an \(\mathcal{O}_{X_1}\)-module. Applying the global-sections functor
1544	  \(\Gamma\) (over the base \(R\)) to the component
1545	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}_M\) of the pushforward-composition coherence
1546	  isomorphism yields the identity morphism:
1547	  \[
1548	    \Gamma\bigl((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}_M\bigr) = \operatorname{id}.
1549	  \]
1550	  The pushforward-composition coherence is transparent at the level of sections.
1551	\end{lemma}
1552	\begin{proof}
1553	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
1554	  The component \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{hom}}_M\) evaluates to the identity
1555	  on every open, and the global-sections functor sends the identity to the identity.
1556	\end{proof}
1557	
1558	\begin{lemma}
1559	
1560	\leanok
1561	[$\Gamma$-collapse of the pushforward-composition coherence (inv factor)]
1562	  \label{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id}
1563	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.gammaMap_pushforwardComp_inv_eq_id}
1564	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}

1566	  \(\Gamma\)-collapse holds for the inverse component:
1567	  \[
1568	    \Gamma\bigl((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{a,b})^{\mathrm{inv}}_M\bigr) = \operatorname{id}.
1569	  \]
1570	\end{lemma}
1571	\begin{proof}
1572	  \uses{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}
1573	  The inverse component evaluates to the identity on every open, and the global-sections
1574	  functor preserves the identity.
1575	\end{proof}

1577	\begin{lemma}
1578	
1579	\leanok

1615	  isomorphism, \(\iota_A : A \to A \otimes_R R'\) the left tensor inclusion, and set
1616	  \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\). For an \(A\)-module \(M\), the bare \((g')\)-unit

1620	      \;=\;

1623	      \,\circ\, (\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{\widetilde M}
1624	      \,\circ\, (g')_{*}\bigl((\mathrm{pullbackComp}_{e,\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A})^{\mathrm{hom}}_{\widetilde M}\bigr),
1625	  \]

1631	  \(b = \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), \(N = \widetilde M\), reads

1641	  Since \((g')_{*}\) is a functor it carries
1642	  \((\mathrm{pullbackComp})^{\mathrm{inv}} \circ (\mathrm{pullbackComp})^{\mathrm{hom}} = \mathrm{id}\)
1643	  to the identity, so the right-hand side collapses to \(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\), giving the stated

1999	  identification \(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}} = \) Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read}.

2003	  \emph{(i) Abstract variable-legs restatement.} Restate the codomain read — and, with it, the
2004	  whole four-factor chain — for \emph{generic} morphisms \(g', f'\) carrying the cone-leg
2005	  equalities \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) and
2006	  \(f' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_{R'}\) as explicit hypotheses, in place of the literal
2007	  projections \(\operatorname{pr}_1, \operatorname{pr}_2\). In this abstract form the legs are
2008	  free variables, not terms pinned by the pullback's universal property, so each can be
2009	  eliminated by substituting its defining equation: the equality proof \(w\), the adjunction
2010	  index, and the \(\gamma_{\psi}\)/\(\Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) arguments are all expressed in the
2011	  free leg variables and transform uniformly under the substitution. This restatement converts
2012	  the blocked rewrite into a legitimate one; it is the structural heart of the step.
2013	
2014	  \emph{(ii) \(\Gamma\)-collapse of the transparent coherences.} On global sections, read through
2015	  the \(\Gamma\)-pushforward dictionaries (Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardTildeIso},
2016	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaPushforwardIso}), the two pushforward-composition factors and the
2017	  congruence factor are transparent. The composition coherences collapse to the identity —
2018	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) and
2019	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{f',g})^{\mathrm{inv}}\) evaluate, on the top open, to the identity
2020	  map on restriction of scalars (their section-level values are the identity) — and they
2021	  serve only to rebracket the iterated pushforward along the two factorizations
2022	  \((g' \circ f)_* = (f' \circ g)_*\) of the square's composite. (The
2023	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardComp}_{g',f})^{\mathrm{hom}}\) collapse is a transparent re-association
2024	  that is most naturally accounted for together with the leg-reindex of step~(iii) rather than at
2025	  this point; the \(\Gamma\)-collapse identity itself is the same.) The congruence factor collapses
2026	  to a canonical transport along the equality of opens: its section-level value
2027	  \((\mathrm{pushforwardCongr}\,w)^{\mathrm{hom}}\) is the
2028	  presheaf-restriction along the canonical equality of opens, i.e.\ a repackaging along
2029	  that equality carrying no substantive content. Thus after the \(\Gamma\)-collapse the only
2030	  surviving non-trivial factor is \(f_{*}\bigl(\eta^{g'}_{\widetilde M}\bigr)\) read on global
2031	  sections.
2032

2034	  (step~(i)) to \(g' = e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\), the leg-reindex engine
2035	  Lemma~\ref{lem:pullbackPushforward_unit_comp}, instantiated at \(a = e\),

2037	  \(\eta^{g'} = \eta^{e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A}\), together with the

2039	  \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit followed by \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)_{*}\) of the

2041	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:pullback_isEquivalence_of_iso}) and the \(e\)-unit is invertible, hence an

2049	  scalars along \(\psi\) of the codomain-read transport of the unit value, namely

2053	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\)'' bookkeeping of the source. Each of (i)--(iii) extracts as a
2054	  named lemma; together they close the reindex without ever rewriting the bare legs.

2377	  The four distributed \(\Gamma\)-image factors are cancelled against the unfolded leg-parametrised
2378	  codomain read Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_codomain_read_legs} by the three one-cancellation
2379	  atoms. Carrying the legs as free parameters and identifying them with
2380	  \(e \circ \operatorname{Spec}\iota_A\) definitionally removes the dependent-motive obstruction that
2381	  walls the literal-leg form, but it does \emph{not} by itself make the atoms applicable by matching
2382	  their head symbol against the locked composite.
2383	
2384	  \emph{The residual obstruction.} After the global-sections functor \(\Gamma\) is distributed over
2385	  the composite, the factor to be collapsed sits over the \emph{nested-image} object \(G(H(M))\) —
2386	  the form produced by distributing a functor over a composite of morphisms — whereas the collapsing
2387	  atom fixes the same object in its \emph{composed-functor} form \((H \circ G)(M)\), the form in
2388	  which the pushforward-composition coherence isomorphism presents its (co)domain. These two object
2389	  presentations are definitionally but not syntactically equal. Consequently a cancellation keyed on
2390	  the atom's head symbol cannot abstract the motive (the pattern is not found), and a
2391	  definitional-equality-tolerant cancellation exhausts the reduction budget on the large concrete
2392	  leg term. The same diamond recurs at each of the three cancellations.
2393	
2394	  \emph{The resolution: single-factor surgery in term mode.} Each factor-equation is lifted into the
2395	  ambient composite \emph{one neighbour at a time}: a factor identity \(u = u'\) is promoted to
2396	  \(c \circ u = c \circ u'\), or to \(u \circ c = u' \circ c\), by congruence in the composition
2397	  operation with the neighbouring factor \(c\) held fixed, and the promoted steps are chained in
2398	  sequence. The chain is then closed on the definitional bridge between the nested-image and
2399	  composed-functor presentations of the shared object; it is at this single closing step that the
2400	  object-presentation and associativity diamond is absorbed — the move a head-symbol cancellation
2401	  structurally cannot make. The three atoms are \emph{already} supplied in the goal's
2402	  \(\Gamma \circ (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_{*}\) form
2403	  (Lemmas~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_eUnit},
2404	  \ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pushforwardComp},
2405	  \ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pullbackComp}); they are spliced directly into the
2406	  composite by this congruence chaining, with no detour through the bare section-level collapse of
2407	  Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id}.
2408	
2409	  \emph{The three cancellations.} Cancellation~(1): the \(e\)-unit is invertible
2410	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_eUnit}) and meets its inverse inside the codomain
2411	  read. Cancellation~(2): the surviving pushforward-composition coherence has identity
2412	  \(\Gamma\)-image (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pushforwardComp}, whose
2413	  section-level content is the collapse Lemma~\ref{lem:gammaMap_pushforwardComp_hom_eq_id}).
2414	  Cancellation~(3): the trailing pullback-comparison factor cancels the inverse the codomain read
2415	  bakes in (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_inner_eCancel_pullbackComp}). The lone survivor is the
2416	  affine \((\operatorname{Spec}\iota_A)\)-unit, evaluated by Seam~1
2417	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_unit_value}) to \(\rho\).
2418	\end{proof}

2493	  \uses{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv, def:base_change_mate_inner_value}
2494	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma `lemma-affine-base-change'

2496	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L933--938).

2499	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base change''
2500	  (proof, ``boils down to the equality'' step).}
2501	  Let \(\rho : M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\), \(m \mapsto (1 \otimes 1) \otimes m\), be the
2502	  inner comparison value of Definition~\ref{def:base_change_mate_inner_value}. The extension of
2503	  scalars along \(\psi : R \to R'\) of \(\rho\), post-composed with the algebraic counit
2504	  \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) of the extension/restriction-of-scalars adjunction along \(\psi\),
2505	  is an \(R'\)-linear map \(R' \otimes_R M \to (A \otimes_R R') \otimes_A M\); on the generator
2506	  \(r' \otimes m\) it returns \(r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) = (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\),
2507	  which is exactly the value of the inverse regrouping isomorphism
2508	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_mate_regroupEquiv}) on \(r' \otimes m\). Both maps being
2509	  \(R'\)-linear and agreeing on the generators \(r' \otimes m\), they coincide:
2510	  \[
2511	    \operatorname{ext}_\psi(\rho) \,\circ\, \varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}
2512	      \;=\; \operatorname{regroup}^{-1}.
2513	  \]
2514	\end{lemma}
2515	\begin{proof}

2517	  Both sides are \(R'\)-linear maps out of \(R' \otimes_R M\), so by extensionality it suffices to
2518	  check the value on the generators \(r' \otimes m\). The left side sends
2519	  \(r' \otimes m \mapsto r' \cdot \rho(m) = r' \cdot ((1 \otimes 1) \otimes m) =
2520	  (1 \otimes r') \otimes m\), using that \(\varepsilon^{\mathrm{alg}}\) reads the extension of
2521	  scalars by acting through the \(R'\)-scalar. The inverse regrouping isomorphism of

2524	  values agree on every generator, so the maps are equal. No flatness hypothesis is used.

308	and using the unit iso `tilde.toTildeΓNatIso` (`Γ(M^~, ⊤) ≅ M`), the `R`-module of global
309	sections of `(Spec φ)_* (M^~)` is the restriction of scalars of `M` along `φ`. This is the

313	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardTildeIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R')
314	    (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') :
315	    (moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R)).obj

321	/-- **Sections of an affine pushforward over an arbitrary open = restriction of scalars.**
322	The `D(a)`-level (indeed arbitrary-open) generalization of `gammaPushforwardIso`. For a ring map
323	`φ : R ⟶ R'`, an `Spec R'`-module `N`, and an open `U` of `Spec R` with preimage
324	`V = (Spec φ)⁻¹ U` in `Spec R'`, the `R`-module of sections of the pushforward
325	`(Spec φ)_* N` over `U` is the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-module of sections of
326	`N` over `V`. Because `modulesSpecToSheaf` forgets to the *global* section ring uniformly (it
327	restricts scalars along the global-sections map at the top open, not at `U`), the construction is
328	*identical* to that of `gammaPushforwardIso` — the same restriction-of-scalars composition identity
329	twice plus the same `⊤`-level ring equation `globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop` — with only
330	the evaluation open changed from `⊤` to `U` / `V`. Project-local. -/
331	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardIsoAt {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R')
332	    (N : (Spec R').Modules) (U : (Spec R).Opens) :
333	    (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj N)).val.obj
334	        (Opposite.op U) ≅
335	      (ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj
336	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj
337	          (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (Spec.map φ).base).obj U))) := by
338	  set SecN : ModuleCat ↑((Spec R').ringCatSheaf.obj.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
339	    (((SheafOfModules.forgetToSheafModuleCat (Spec R').ringCatSheaf (Opposite.op ⊤)
340	        (Limits.initialOpOfTerminal Limits.isTerminalTop)).obj N).val.obj
341	          (Opposite.op ((TopologicalSpace.Opens.map (Spec.map φ).base).obj U))) with hSecN
342	  set gsRhom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R).hom.hom with hgsR
343	  set gsR'hom := (StructureSheaf.globalSectionsIso ↑R').hom.hom with hgsR'
344	  set pushTop := ((Spec.map φ).toRingCatSheafHom.hom.app (Opposite.op ⊤)).hom with hpush
345	  have hcomp : pushTop.comp gsRhom = gsR'hom.comp φ.hom := by
346	    apply RingHom.ext
347	    intro x
348	    simpa [hpush, hgsR, hgsR', RingHom.comp_apply] using
349	      congr($(globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop φ).hom x)
350	  exact (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App gsRhom pushTop (pushTop.comp gsRhom) rfl SecN).symm ≪≫
351	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr hcomp).app SecN ≪≫
352	    (ModuleCat.restrictScalarsComp'App φ.hom gsR'hom (gsR'hom.comp φ.hom) rfl SecN)

356	/-- **Counit of the tilde–Γ adjunction is a basic-open isomorphism whenever the restriction
357	of the global sections is a localization.** For a sheaf of modules `N` on `Spec R` and `a : R`,
358	if the structure-sheaf restriction map `Γ(N, ⊤) → Γ(N, D(a))` (read in `ModuleCat R` via the
359	global-sections forgetful functor `modulesSpecToSheaf`) exhibits `Γ(N, D(a))` as the localization
360	of `Γ(N, ⊤)` at `Submonoid.powers a`, then the counit `fromTildeΓ N` is an isomorphism on the
361	sections over `D(a)`. This is the section-level engine of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`: it isolates
362	the *only* nontrivial input (`Γ(N, D(a)) = Γ(N, ⊤)[1/a]`) and discharges the rest — the comparison
363	`fromTildeΓ` is, on `D(a)`, the canonical map between two localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)` (the tilde
364	localization `toOpen` and the restriction), hence an isomorphism by the uniqueness of localized
365	modules. Project-local: Mathlib has the localization-uniqueness lemmas but not this packaging at
366	the level of `fromTildeΓ`. -/
367	lemma fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule {R : CommRingCat.{u}} (N : (Spec R).Modules)
368	    (a : R)
369	    [IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
370	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map
371	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom] :
372	    IsIso (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app N.fromTildeΓ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)) := by
373	  -- The underlying function of `Hom.app` agrees (by `rfl`) with that of the `modulesSpecToSheaf`
374	  -- image, where the tilde–Γ triangle identity `toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app` applies.
375	  rw [ConcreteCategory.isIso_iff_bijective]
376	  have hfun : ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
377	        (Scheme.Modules.Hom.app N.fromTildeΓ (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))) =
378	      ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom
379	        ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)))) :=
380	    rfl
381	  rw [hfun]
382	  -- Abbreviations: `j` is the tilde localization map, `ρ` the structure-sheaf restriction, both
383	  -- localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)` at `powers a`; `L` is the section map of the counit.
384	  set j := (tilde.toOpen ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤))
385	    (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)).hom with hj
386	  set ρ := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj N).val.map
387	    (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom with hρ
388	  haveI hρinst : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a) ρ := by rw [hρ]; infer_instance
389	  clear_value ρ
390	  set L := ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app
391	    (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a))).hom with hL
392	  -- The triangle identity gives `L ∘ₗ j = ρ`.
393	  have htri : L ∘ₗ j = ρ := by
394	    have := Scheme.Modules.toOpen_fromTildeΓ_app N (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)
395	    apply_fun ModuleCat.Hom.hom at this
396	    simpa [hL, hj, hρ, ModuleCat.hom_comp] using this
397	  -- `L` equals the canonical iso between the two localizations of `Γ(N, ⊤)`, hence bijective.
398	  set ej := IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers a) j with hej
399	  set eρ := IsLocalizedModule.iso (Submonoid.powers a) ρ with heρ
400	  let e := ej.symm.trans eρ
401	  have hcomp : e.toLinearMap ∘ₗ j = ρ := by
402	    ext x
403	    simp only [e, hej, heρ, LinearMap.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, LinearEquiv.coe_coe,
404	      LinearEquiv.trans_apply, IsLocalizedModule.iso_symm_apply]
405	    exact IsLocalizedModule.iso_mk_one (Submonoid.powers a) ρ x
406	  have hLeq : L = e.toLinearMap :=
407	    IsLocalizedModule.ext (Submonoid.powers a) j (IsLocalizedModule.map_units ρ)
408	      (htri.trans hcomp.symm)
409	  rw [show ⇑(ConcreteCategory.hom ((modulesSpecToSheaf.map N.fromTildeΓ).1.app
410	    (Opposite.op (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a)))) = ⇑L from rfl, hLeq]
411	  exact e.bijective

413	/-- **Affine pushforward of a tilde-module, conditional on the basic-open localization fact.**
414	This is the full assembly of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` (route iii), modulo the single
415	remaining ingredient: that on each basic open `D(a)` the structure-sheaf restriction of the
416	pushforward `(Spec φ)_* M^~` exhibits its sections over `D(a)` as the localization at
417	`Submonoid.powers a` of its global sections. Given that hypothesis (`hloc`), the counit
418	`fromTildeΓ ((Spec φ)_* M^~)` is an isomorphism (basis-locality criterion
419	`Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis` over the basic opens, with each section iso supplied by
420	`fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule`), so `(Spec φ)_* M^~` lies in the essential image of
421	`tilde` and the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardTildeIso` upgrades to the desired
422	object isomorphism.
423	
424	The hypothesis `hloc` is the *only* outstanding obligation for the unconditional
425	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`: it is the affine "`(Spec φ)⁻¹ D(a) = D(φ a)`, and localizing
426	`restrict φ M` at `a` agrees with localizing `M` at `φ a`" fact (Stacks, widetilde-pullback),
427	whose ring-change core is `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` below. Discharging it
428	requires identifying the `modulesSpecToSheaf` global-ring `R`-action on the sections of the
429	pushforward over `D(a)` with restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-localization
430	`M[1/φ a]` — the section-level structure-sheaf naturality at `D(a)`. -/
431	noncomputable def pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
432	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R')
433	    (hloc : ∀ a : R, IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers a)
434	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M))).val.map
435	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom) :
436	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
437	      tilde ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by
438	  have hiso : IsIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ
439	      ((Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M))) := by
440	    apply Modules.isIso_of_isIso_app_of_isBasis
441	      (B := fun a : R => PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen a) PrimeSpectrum.isBasis_basic_opens
442	    intro a
443	    haveI := hloc a
444	    exact fromTildeΓ_app_isIso_of_isLocalizedModule _ a
445	  exact (asIso (Scheme.Modules.fromTildeΓ _)).symm ≪≫
446	    (tilde.functor R).mapIso (gammaPushforwardTildeIso φ M)

448	/-- **Restriction of scalars of a localized module is a localized module.** If `f : M →ₗ[A] N`
449	exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at the image submonoid `Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S`
450	(for `S : Submonoid R` and `A` an `R`-algebra), then the `R`-linear map underlying `f`
451	exhibits `N` as the localization of `M` at `S` itself. This is the exact converse of
452	`IsLocalizedModule.of_restrictScalars` and is the ring-change ingredient powering the affine
453	pushforward identification: localizing `restrictScalars φ M` at `a ∈ R` agrees with localizing
454	`M` at `φ a ∈ R'`. Project-local: Mathlib ships only the forward direction. -/
455	lemma IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars
456	    {R A : Type*} [CommSemiring R] [CommSemiring A] [Algebra R A]
457	    {M N : Type*} [AddCommMonoid M] [AddCommMonoid N]
458	    [Module R M] [Module R N] [Module A M] [Module A N]
459	    [IsScalarTower R A M] [IsScalarTower R A N]
460	    (S : Submonoid R) (f : M →ₗ[A] N)
461	    [IsLocalizedModule (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f] :
462	    IsLocalizedModule S (f.restrictScalars R) where
463	  map_units x := by
464	    have h := IsLocalizedModule.map_units f
465	      (⟨algebraMap R A x, Submonoid.mem_map.mpr ⟨x, x.2, rfl⟩⟩ : Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S)
466	    simp only [← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_apply, Module.End.isUnit_iff] at h ⊢
467	    exact h
468	  surj y := by
469	    obtain ⟨⟨x, ⟨_, t, ht, rfl⟩⟩, e⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) f y
470	    exact ⟨⟨x, ⟨t, ht⟩⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using e⟩
471	  exists_of_eq {x₁ x₂} e := by
472	    obtain ⟨⟨_, c, hc, rfl⟩, h⟩ :=
473	      IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq (S := Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid A S) (f := f) e
474	    exact ⟨⟨c, hc⟩, by simpa [Submonoid.smul_def, IsScalarTower.algebraMap_smul] using h⟩
475	
476	/-- **The structure-sheaf restriction of a tilde-module from `⊤` to `D(b)` is a localization.**
477	Read in `ModuleCat R'` via `modulesSpecToSheaf`, the restriction map
478	`Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(b))` exhibits its target as the localization of its source at
479	`Submonoid.powers b`. This packages the Mathlib instance
480	`tilde.toOpen … (basicOpen b)` (which makes `M → Γ(M^~, D(b))` a localization) together with the
481	triangle identity `tilde.toOpen_res` and the fact that `M → Γ(M^~, ⊤)` is bijective (localization
482	at the trivial submonoid `powers 1`). Project-local. -/
483	lemma tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule {R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') (b : R') :
484	    IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers b)
485	      ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
486	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom := by
487	  -- `toOpen M ⊤` is a localization at `powers (1 : R')` (since `basicOpen 1 = ⊤`), hence bijective.
488	  haveI inst1 : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
489	    have h := (inferInstance : IsLocalizedModule (Submonoid.powers (1 : R'))
490	      (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (1 : R'))).hom)
491	    rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one] at h
492	    exact h
493	  have hbij : Function.Bijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom := by
494	    refine ⟨fun x y hxy => ?_, fun y => ?_⟩
495	    · obtain ⟨c, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.exists_of_eq
496	        (S := Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (f := (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom) hxy
497	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
498	      have hc1 : (↑c : R') = 1 := by simpa using hn.symm
499	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def, Submonoid.smul_def, hc1, one_smul, one_smul] at hc
500	      exact hc
501	    · obtain ⟨⟨x, c⟩, hc⟩ := IsLocalizedModule.surj
502	        (Submonoid.powers (1 : R')) (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom y
503	      obtain ⟨n, hn⟩ := c.2
504	      have hc1 : (↑c : R') = 1 := by simpa using hn.symm
505	      refine ⟨x, ?_⟩
506	      rw [Submonoid.smul_def, hc1, one_smul] at hc
507	      exact hc.symm
508	  -- Triangle identity: `toOpen ⊤ ≫ (restriction) = toOpen (D b)`.
509	  have htri := tilde.toOpen_res M (⊤ : (Spec R').Opens) (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)
510	    (homOfLE (le_top))
511	  -- The inverse equivalence `Γ(M^~, ⊤) ≃ M`.
512	  set le : M ≃ₗ[R'] ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.obj (Opposite.op ⊤)) :=
513	    LinearEquiv.ofBijective (tilde.toOpen M ⊤).hom hbij with hle
514	  -- The triangle at the level of linear maps: `restriction ∘ₗ le = toOpen (D b)`.
515	  have htri2 : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
516	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom ∘ₗ le.toLinearMap
517	      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom := by
518	    have h := congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom htri
519	    rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp] at h
520	    exact h
521	  -- Hence `restriction = toOpen (D b) ∘ₗ le⁻¹`, a localization at `powers b` precomposed with
522	  -- a linear equivalence.
523	  have key : ((modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
524	        (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op).hom
525	      = (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom ∘ₗ le.symm.toLinearMap :=
526	    (LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm _ _).mpr htri2
527	  rw [key]
528	  exact IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (Submonoid.powers b)
529	    (tilde.toOpen M (PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen b)).hom le.symm

531	/-- **Affine pushforward of a tilde-module (unconditional).** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'`
532	and an `R'`-module `M`, pushing the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` forward along `Spec φ` is,
533	up to canonical isomorphism, the tilde of the restriction of scalars of `M` along `φ`. This
534	is the unconditional form of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule`: the latter's
535	hypothesis `hloc` (each structure-sheaf restriction `Γ(N,⊤) → Γ(N,D(a))` is a localization at
536	`powers a`) is discharged here by the `D(a)`-level transport. See blueprint
537	`lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
538	noncomputable def pushforward_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
539	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R') :
540	    (Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
541	      tilde ((ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom).obj M) := by
542	  apply pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule φ M
543	  intro a
544	  -- STRATEGY (`of_linearEquiv`): the restriction map `ρ : Γ(N,⊤) → Γ(N,D(a))` of
545	  -- `N := (Spec φ)_* (M^~)` is, under the isomorphisms
546	  -- `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) ⊤` and `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) (D a)`,
547	  -- identified with the restriction of scalars along `φ` of the `R'`-side tilde restriction
548	  -- `σ : Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(φ a))`. That `σ` is a localization at `powers (φ a)`
549	  -- (`tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`), so its restriction of scalars along `φ` is a
550	  -- localization at `powers a` (`IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars`); transporting along
551	  -- the two isomorphisms then yields `hloc(a)`.
552	  -- The two bricks for this discharge are now in place and axiom-clean:
553	  --   • `gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) U : Γ(N, U) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ(M^~, (Spec φ)⁻¹ U))`
554	  --     — the open-indexed `e_{D(a)}` isomorphism (blueprint movement (1)); and
555	  --   • `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule M (φ a)` — the `R'`-side restriction
556	  --     `Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(φ a))` is a localization at `powers (φ a)`.
557	  -- The `of_linearEquiv` finish is: with `e₁ = gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) ⊤` and
558	  -- `e₂ = gammaPushforwardIsoAt φ (tilde M) (D a)`, the naturality square
559	  -- `e₂.hom ∘ ρ = (restrictScalars φ σ) ∘ e₁.hom` (where `σ` is the `R'`-side restriction and
560	  -- `ρ` the pushforward-side one) gives `ρ = e₂.symm ∘ₗ (restrictScalars φ σ) ∘ₗ e₁`; then
561	  -- `IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` upgrades `σ` (localization at `powers (φ a)`,
562	  -- since `algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a) = powers (φ a)` along `φ.hom.toAlgebra`) to a
563	  -- localization at `powers a` of `restrictScalars φ σ`, and two applications of
564	  -- `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv` / `of_linearEquiv_right` transport this to `ρ`.
565	  --
566	  -- iter-240 PIVOT (`algebraize`): preimage opens are *definitionally* the basic opens
567	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.SpecMap_preimage_basicOpen` is `rfl`), so the `R'`-side restriction
568	  -- `σ` is literally `tilde M`'s restriction `⊤ → D(φ a)`, and `algebraize [φ.hom]` supplies the
569	  -- honest `Algebra ↑R ↑R'`/`IsScalarTower` instances that `powers_restrictScalars` needs.
570	  algebraize [φ.hom]
571	  -- `σ` : the `R'`-side restriction map `Γ(M^~, ⊤) → Γ(M^~, D(φ a))` of `tilde M`.
572	  set σmor := (modulesSpecToSheaf.obj (tilde M)).val.map
573	      (homOfLE (show PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen (φ.hom a) ≤ ⊤ from le_top)).op with hσmor

657	/-- **Naturality of the Γ-fragment comparison.** The per-object isomorphisms
658	`gammaPushforwardIso φ N : Γ_R((Spec φ)_* N) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ_{R'} N)` assemble into a
659	natural isomorphism of functors `(Spec R').Modules ⥤ ModuleCat R`
660	\[ (\operatorname{Spec}\varphi)_* \;\circ\; \Gamma_R
661	   \;\cong\; \Gamma_{R'} \;\circ\; \operatorname{restr}_\varphi . \]
662	Every constituent of `gammaPushforwardIso` is the identity on underlying elements (the
663	`restrictScalarsComp'App` isos and `restrictScalarsCongr` merely repackage the module structure
664	on the unchanged carrier), so naturality is a pointwise `rfl`. This is the right-adjoint natural
665	isomorphism that drives the affine pullback dictionary `pullback_spec_tilde_iso` via
666	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso`. Project-local. -/
667	noncomputable def gammaPushforwardNatIso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}} (φ : R ⟶ R') :
668	    Scheme.Modules.pushforward (Spec.map φ) ⋙ moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R) ≅
669	      moduleSpecΓFunctor (R := R') ⋙ ModuleCat.restrictScalars φ.hom :=
670	  NatIso.ofComponents (fun N => gammaPushforwardIso φ N) (by
671	    intro N N' g
672	    ext x
673	    rfl)
674	
675	/-- **Affine pullback of a tilde-module.** For a ring map `φ : R ⟶ R'` and an `R`-module `M`,
676	pulling the quasi-coherent sheaf `M^~` back along `Spec φ` is, up to canonical isomorphism, the
677	tilde of the extension of scalars `R' ⊗_R M`. This is the pullback companion of
678	`pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` and is part (1) of Stacks Tag 01I9 (`lemma-widetilde-pullback`).
679	
680	The construction is the uniqueness-of-left-adjoints route. Pullback along `Spec φ` is left adjoint
681	to pushforward, and `tilde` is left adjoint to global sections; their composite
682	`tilde_R ⋙ pullback` is left adjoint to `pushforward ⋙ Γ_R`. Symmetrically `extendScalars φ` is
683	left adjoint to `restrictScalars φ`, so `extendScalars ⋙ tilde_{R'}` is left adjoint to
684	`Γ_{R'} ⋙ restrictScalars φ`. The two right adjoints are identified by the natural isomorphism
685	`gammaPushforwardNatIso` (which is the global-sections comparison `gammaPushforwardIso`), so
686	`Adjunction.natIsoOfRightAdjointNatIso` yields an isomorphism of the two left adjoints; evaluating
687	at `M` gives the claim. Project-local: the affine companion of the pushforward dictionary. See
688	blueprint `lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso`. -/
689	noncomputable def pullback_spec_tilde_iso {R R' : CommRingCat.{u}}
690	    (φ : R ⟶ R') (M : ModuleCat.{u} R) :
691	    (Scheme.Modules.pullback (Spec.map φ)).obj (tilde M) ≅
692	      tilde ((ModuleCat.extendScalars φ.hom).obj M) :=
693	  let adjL := (tilde.adjunction (R := R)).comp
694	    (Scheme.Modules.pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (Spec.map φ))
695	  let adjR := (ModuleCat.extendRestrictScalarsAdj φ.hom).comp (tilde.adjunction (R := R'))
696	  (((conjugateIsoEquiv adjL adjR).symm (gammaPushforwardNatIso φ)).symm).app M
697

2948	  same chain once \(\Theta_{\mathrm{src}}, \Theta_{\mathrm{tgt}}\) are formed; the
2949	  formalized decl records the \(\mathrm{IsIso}\ \Gamma(\alpha)\) corollary, which is
2950	  what the affine close consumes.)
2951	\end{proof}

2953	\begin{lemma}\leanok
2954	[Affine base change]
2955	  \label{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}
2956	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso}
2957	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:base_change_map_affine_local,
2958	        lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso,
2959	        lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange,
2960	        lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso}
2961	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma
2962	  % `lemma-affine-base-change' (\S\,Cohomology and base change, I)
2963	  % (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L906--918).
2964	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Let $f : X \to S$ be a morphism of schemes.
2965	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module.
2966	  % Assume $f$ is affine.
2967	  % In this case $f_*\mathcal{F} \cong Rf_*\mathcal{F}$ is
2968	  % a quasi-coherent sheaf, and for every base change diagram
2969	  % (\ref{equation-base-change-diagram})
2970	  % we have
2971	  % $$
2972	  % g^*f_*\mathcal{F} = f'_*(g')^*\mathcal{F}.
2973	  % $$"
2974	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Cohomology of Schemes, Lemma ``Affine base
2975	  change''.}
2976	  Assume \(f : X \to S\) is an affine morphism and \(\mathcal{F}\) is a
2977	  quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module. Then for every base-change square as
2978	  above the canonical map of
2979	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism
2980	  \[
2981	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\cong\; f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr).
2982	  \]
2983	  The quasi-coherence hypothesis on \(\mathcal{F}\) is essential and is not a mere
2984	  convenience: the proof works over an affine open by writing
2985	  \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\) for a module \(M\), and a general
2986	  \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module need not be of this form
2987	  \(\widetilde{M}\); without it neither the affine description of the pushforward
2988	  nor the tensor-associativity computation below is available, and the conclusion
2989	  fails.
2990	\end{lemma}

2992	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "The vanishing of higher direct images is
2993	% Lemma \ref{lemma-relative-affine-vanishing}.
2994	% The statement is local on $S$ and $S'$. Hence we may
2995	% assume $X = \Spec(A)$, $S = \Spec(R)$,
2996	% $S' = \Spec(R')$ and $\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}$
2997	% for some $A$-module $M$.
2998	% We use Schemes, Lemma \ref{schemes-lemma-widetilde-pullback}
2999	% to describe pullbacks and pushforwards of $\mathcal{F}$.
3000	% Namely, $X' = \Spec(R' \otimes_R A)$ and
3001	% $\mathcal{F}'$ is the quasi-coherent sheaf associated

3003	% Thus we see that the lemma boils down to the
3004	% equality
3005	% $$
3006	% (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M = R' \otimes_R M
3007	% $$
3008	% as $R'$-modules."
3009	% NOTE: This affine lemma is closed directly on global sections, via the two named
3010	% obligations: lem:base_change_map_affine_local (locality reduction on the affine
3011	% opens of S') and lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange (the section-
3012	% level value of the base-change map is cancelBaseChange). Both proved affine tilde
3013	% dictionaries -- lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso and lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso --
3014	% feed the second obligation. cancelBaseChange is in Mathlib with no flatness; the
3015	% affine case carries no flatness hypothesis. No Čech infrastructure is used here.
3016	\begin{proof}
3017	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens, lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso, lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso, lem:base_change_map_affine_local, lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}
3018	  The proof is the composite of the two named obligations, taken \emph{directly on
3019	  global sections}.
3020	
3021	  \medskip
3022	  \emph{First reduction (locality on \(S'\)).} By
3023	  Lemma~\ref{lem:modules_isIso_iff_affineOpens} it suffices to prove that the
3024	  base-change map of Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an
3025	  isomorphism on sections over every affine open of \(S'\). This is exactly the
3026	  locality reduction Lemma~\ref{lem:base_change_map_affine_local}: restricting the
3027	  cartesian square to an affine open \(U = \operatorname{Spec}(R') \subseteq S'\) and
3028	  a chosen affine \(\operatorname{Spec}(R) \subseteq S\) containing \(g(U)\), the
3029	  hypothesis \([\mathrm{IsAffineHom}\ f]\) makes \(X\) restrict to
3030	  \(\operatorname{Spec}(A)\) and quasi-coherence of \(\mathcal{F}\) makes it restrict
3031	  to \(\widetilde{M}\) for an \(A\)-module \(M\). (It is here that quasi-coherence is
3032	  used: it is what licenses writing \(\mathcal{F} = \widetilde{M}\).) Computing the
3033	  fibre product gives \(X' = \operatorname{Spec}(R' \otimes_R A)\) and
3034	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\) the tilde of the \((R' \otimes_R A)\)-module
3035	  \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\). The matter is thus reduced to the affine--affine
3036	  model, where the base-change map is a morphism \(\alpha\) of quasi-coherent
3037	  \((\operatorname{Spec} R')\)-modules.
3038	
3039	  \medskip
3040	  \emph{Passage to global sections.} The functor
3041	  \(\widetilde{(-)} : \operatorname{ModuleCat}(R') \to
3042	  (\operatorname{Spec} R').\mathrm{Modules}\) is fully faithful with essential image
3043	  the quasi-coherent modules; equivalently the counit
3044	  \(\widetilde{\Gamma(\mathcal{N})} \to \mathcal{N}\) is an isomorphism exactly when
3045	  \(\mathcal{N}\) is quasi-coherent. Source and target of \(\alpha\) are
3046	  quasi-coherent, so both counits are isomorphisms and, by their naturality,
3047	  \[
3048	    \mathrm{IsIso}\,\alpha
3049	    \;\Longleftrightarrow\;
3050	    \mathrm{IsIso}\bigl(\Gamma(\alpha)\bigr),
3051	  \]
3052	  where \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is a concrete \(R'\)-linear map in
3053	  \(\operatorname{ModuleCat}(R')\). The whole computation therefore stays at the level
3054	  of modules.
3055	
3056	  \medskip
3057	  \emph{Section-level identification (the second obligation).} By the section-level
3058	  computation Lemma~\ref{lem:pushforward_base_change_mate_cancelBaseChange}, the map
3059	  \(\Gamma(\alpha)\), with its domain \(\Gamma(g^*(f_*\widetilde{M}))\) identified with
3060	  \(R' \otimes_R M\) and its codomain \(\Gamma(f'_*(g')^*\widetilde{M})\) identified
3061	  with \((R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M\) through the two dictionaries
3062	  Lemmas~\ref{lem:pullback_spec_tilde_iso} and~\ref{lem:pushforward_spec_tilde_iso},
3063	  \emph{is} the canonical cancellation isomorphism
3064	  \[
3065	    \operatorname{cancelBaseChange} :
3066	    (R' \otimes_R A) \otimes_A M \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\; R' \otimes_R M,
3067	    \qquad (r' \otimes a) \otimes m \;\mapsto\; r' \otimes (a \cdot m)
3068	  \]
3069	  of \(R'\)-modules (Lemma~\ref{lem:cancelBaseChange_mathlib}). This is the entire
3070	  content of the affine close, and it is performed \emph{directly on the module of
3071	  global sections} — there is no separate abstract adjoint-mate identification at the
3072	  level of sheaves. Because \(\operatorname{cancelBaseChange}\) is an isomorphism with
3073	  no flatness hypothesis whatsoever, \(\Gamma(\alpha)\) is an isomorphism, hence so is
3074	  \(\alpha\) on the affine chart, and by the locality reduction so is the global
3075	  base-change map. Flatness of \(g\) plays no role in the affine lemma; it enters only
3076	  in the global statement Theorem~\ref{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}, where it is
3077	  used to commute \(- \otimes_A B\) with the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\).
3078	\end{proof}

3080	\section{Flat base change for the pushforward}
3081	
3082	The global statement is assembled from the affine lemma and the single homological
3083	input that flatness preserves the finite equalizer computing \(H^0\). That input is
3084	supplied verbatim by Mathlib, recorded here as a dependency anchor.
3085	
3086	\begin{lemma}[Flat base change preserves finite equalizers]
3087	  \label{lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
3088	  \lean{LinearMap.tensorEqLocusEquiv}
3089	  \mathlibok
3090	  \textit{Provided by Mathlib.}
3091	  Let \(A \to B\) be a flat ring homomorphism (more generally, let \(B\) be a flat
3092	  \(A\)-module that is also an \(A\)-algebra, with the scalar-tower compatibility),
3093	  and let \(f, g : N \rightrightarrows P\) be a pair of \(A\)-linear maps. Write
3094	  \(\operatorname{eq}(f, g) = \{x \in N : f(x) = g(x)\}\) for their equalizer. Then the
3095	  canonical \(B\)-linear comparison map
3096	  \[
3097	    B \otimes_A \operatorname{eq}(f, g)
3098	    \;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;
3099	    \operatorname{eq}\bigl(\mathbf{1}_B \otimes f,\ \mathbf{1}_B \otimes g\bigr)
3100	  \]
3101	  is an isomorphism; that is, base change along a flat algebra commutes with the
3102	  formation of the finite equalizer (equivalently, of the kernel: the case
3103	  \(g = 0\) is \texttt{LinearMap.tensorKerEquiv}, underlain by
3104	  \texttt{Module.Flat.ker\_lTensor\_eq} and \texttt{Module.Flat.eqLocus\_lTensor\_eq}).
3105	  Since \(- \otimes_A B\) also preserves finite products, it follows that
3106	  \(- \otimes_A B\) carries the degree-\(0\)/\(1\) equalizer
3107	  \(\prod_i \Gamma(U_i, \mathcal{F}) \rightrightarrows
3108	    \prod_{i,j} \Gamma(U_{ij}, \mathcal{F})\) of a finite cover to the corresponding
3109	  equalizer of the base-changed cover.
3110	\end{lemma}
3111	
3112	\begin{theorem}\leanok
3113	[Flat base change, $i=0$ case]
3114	  \label{thm:flat_base_change_pushforward}
3115	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso}
3116	  \uses{def:pushforward_base_change_map, lem:affine_base_change_pushforward,
3117	        lem:flat_preserves_equalizer_mathlib}
3118	  % SOURCE: [Stacks Project], Tag 02KH (Lemma "Flat base change", part for
3119	  % $i = 0$) (read from references/stacks-coherent.tex, L947--970, and
3120	  % references/stacks-coherent.md).
3121	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "Consider a cartesian diagram of schemes
3122	  % $$
3123	  % \xymatrix{
3124	  % X' \ar[d]_{f'} \ar[r]_{g'} & X \ar[d]^f \\
3125	  % S' \ar[r]^g & S
3126	  % }
3127	  % $$
3128	  % Let $\mathcal{F}$ be a quasi-coherent $\mathcal{O}_X$-module
3129	  % with pullback $\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}$.
3130	  % Assume that $g$ is flat and that $f$ is quasi-compact and quasi-separated.
3131	  % For any $i \geq 0$
3132	  % \begin{enumerate}
3133	  % \item the base change map of
3134	  % Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-base-change-map-flat-case}
3135	  % is an isomorphism
3136	  % $$
3137	  % g^*R^if_*\mathcal{F} \longrightarrow R^if'_*\mathcal{F}',
3138	  % $$
3139	  % \item if $S = \Spec(A)$ and $S' = \Spec(B)$, then
3140	  % $H^i(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B = H^i(X', \mathcal{F}')$.
3141	  % \end{enumerate}"
3142	  \textit{Source: Stacks Project, Tag 02KH (``Flat base change''), the
3143	  \(i = 0\) case.}
3144	  Consider the cartesian square
3145	  \[
3146	    \begin{array}{ccc}
3147	      X' & \xrightarrow{\;g'\;} & X \\[2pt]
3148	      {\scriptstyle f'}\big\downarrow & & \big\downarrow{\scriptstyle f} \\[2pt]
3149	      S' & \xrightarrow{\;g\;} & S
3150	    \end{array}
3151	  \]
3152	  with \(\mathcal{F}\) a quasi-coherent \(\mathcal{O}_X\)-module and
3153	  \(\mathcal{F}' = (g')^*\mathcal{F}\). Assume that \(g\) is flat and that \(f\)
3154	  is quasi-compact and quasi-separated. Then the base-change map of
3155	  Definition~\ref{def:pushforward_base_change_map} is an isomorphism
3156	  \[
3157	    g^*\bigl(f_*\mathcal{F}\bigr) \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
3158	    f'_*\bigl((g')^*\mathcal{F}\bigr) = f'_*\mathcal{F}' .
3159	  \]
3160	  Equivalently, in the affine situation \(S = \operatorname{Spec}(A)\),
3161	  \(S' = \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) with \(A \to B\) flat, the comparison map
3162	  \[
3163	    H^0(X, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B \;\xrightarrow{\;\sim\;}\;
3164	    H^0(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B)
3165	  \]
3166	  is an isomorphism, where \(X_B = X \times_{\operatorname{Spec}(A)}
3167	  \operatorname{Spec}(B)\) and \(\mathcal{F}_B\) is the pullback of
3168	  \(\mathcal{F}\).
3169	
3170	  The hypothesis that \(\mathcal{F}\) is quasi-coherent is essential: it is what
3171	  makes \(f_*\mathcal{F}\) quasi-coherent on the affine base and thus the tilde of
3172	  a module, and it is what lets the affine reduction
3173	  (Lemma~\ref{lem:affine_base_change_pushforward}) write \(\mathcal{F}\) as
3174	  \(\widetilde{M}\) over each affine open. Without it the comparison map need not be
3175	  an isomorphism.
3176	\end{theorem}

3215	% by base change, then it is still the case that each $(U_i)_B$ is affine
3216	% and that $X_B$ is separated. Thus we obtain
3217	% $$
3218	% \check{H}^p(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) = H^p(X_B, \mathcal{F}_B).
3219	% $$

3221	% $$
3222	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}_B, \mathcal{F}_B) =
3223	% \check{\mathcal{C}}^\bullet(\mathcal{U}, \mathcal{F}) \otimes_A B
3224	% $$

3226	% Lemma \ref{lemma-affine-base-change}.

3228	%

3230	% $\mathcal{U} : X = U_1 \cup \ldots \cup U_t$. We will use the
3231	% {\v C}ech-to-cohomology spectral sequence
3232	% Cohomology, Lemma \ref{cohomology-lemma-cech-spectral-sequence}.
3233	% The reader who wishes to avoid this spectral sequence
3234	% can use Mayer-Vietoris and induction on $t$ as in the proof of
